stories.headline: Half a billion for the gunslinger — Mahomes signs the richest paper in league history
stories.read: Kansas City just hitched two more years onto Patrick Mahomes' deal, runnin' clear through 2033 and totin' up $504.75 million — the first contract in NFL history to clear half a billion. Now hold up, money don't score points, pardner. But read the trail sign: comin' off a lost season, the herd's lettin' the best gunslinger of his generation slide at the saloon while the ranch just bet the whole spread he's still that fella. If he drifts past the early rounds of your land grab, you grab them reins and don't look back.
stories.source: straight from the wire at NFL.com

stories.headline: Showdown in Cleveland: Watson and Shedeur split the reins and nobody's blinkin'
stories.read: Out at Browns minicamp they're splittin' the first-team reins clean down the middle between Deshaun Watson and young Shedeur Sanders, and boss man Todd Monken says he ain't namin' a starter till the preseason dust settles. Watson threw a pick-six and a deep strike on the same afternoon — that's a wagon I ain't bettin' on yet. Here's your play: Sanders is cheap gold sittin' in unclaimed territory, and whoever wins this showdown sets the price on Jerry Jeudy and every critter catchin' passes by the lake.
stories.source: the camp scouts over at CBS Sports

stories.headline: Alec Pierce laid up till late camp — free land openin' up in Indy
stories.read: Word out of Indianapolis is Pierce went under the knife on that ankle back in March, got handed a four-to-six month timeline, and won't ride again till late trainin' camp. Every snap he misses is free land, and Josh Downs and Adonai Mitchell are already out there drivin' stakes. Pierce was the deep-ball critter in that outfit — if he comes back slow, that downfield haul gets divvied up fresh, and the saloon ain't repriced a lick of it yet.
